sources.notes: Statement explicitly says this is an interactive problem: information for vertex x is given only after the answer for S(x+1), and each line must be flushed.
Official editorial gives the LCM/GCD bottom-up greedy DP and prints/flushed answers after each node.
Luogu article confirms the forced-online setting and the LCM of possible child path GCDs.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution page; not used because this problem is excluded from ordinary searchable scope.

annotation.summary: Excluded from the normal searchable ICPC-style database: this is a technically interactive streaming-output problem, not ordinary batch input/output.
annotation.constraints: The judge supplies node data from n down to 1 only after reading the previous output line; each answer must be printed and flushed online.
annotation.core_idea: Algorithmically, one can process nodes bottom-up. Let dp[u] be the minimum path count for the known subtree, and val[u] be the LCM of possible non-1 GCD values of an upward path through u. For each child c, if gcd(val[c], a_u) > 1 then u can attach to a child path; combine such gcds by LCM. If no child accepts u, open a new path and set val[u]=a_u. However, this solution relies on online I/O order and flushing, so it is outside this repository's default standard AC searchable scope.
annotation.complexity: Excluded; algorithmic solution is O(n log A) but requires special online interactive I/O.
